intro: Polygala hookeri, also known as Hooker's milkwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Polygala hookeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Polygala hookeri is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as syrups and capsules.
